_metadata.description: Ingredients backed by clinical research and studies - Enhanced for Section C v3.4.0
changelog: 2.5 (2026-02-20): Added 4 high-impact clinical entries (whey protein, generic citicoline, alpha-GPC, Saccharomyces boulardii strain), upgraded taurine from preclinical to ingredient-human systematic-review evidence, and added brand/generic guardrails for Cognizin aliases. Total entries: 176

2.4 (2026-02-20): Added 8 high-impact clinical records (generic creatine monohydrate, peppermint oil, LGG/BB-12/HN019 strains, GABA, chamomile, beetroot) and aligned 19 systematic-review entries to study_type=systematic_review_meta for score consistency. Total entries: 172

2.3 (2026-02-17): Evidence-quality cleanup pass removed 6 weak/inconclusive entries, renamed PRECLIN_PEA -> INGR_PEA, and downgraded study_type for low-certainty nutrient/herbal records. Total entries: 164
2.2 (2026-02-17): Added 33 clinically reviewed ingredient records (branded, generic, and emerging), expanded elderberry alias coverage for exact-match evidence mapping. Total entries: 170
2.1 (2026-01-05): Added 12 new clinically-backed branded ingredients (Nitrosigine, LJ100, Shoden, Testofen, Magtein, Sunfiber, Cynatine HNS, TamaFlex, HMB, Zynamite, HEAL9). Total entries: 137
2.0 (2025-12-04): Added study_type field support, updated evidence_level mapping for hierarchy scoring

evidence_level_values.product-human: Exact product tested in human RCTs (1.0x multiplier)
evidence_level_values.branded-rct: Branded ingredient with own RCTs (0.8x multiplier) - e.g., KSM-66, Meriva

study_type_values.systematic_review_meta: Meta-analysis or systematic review (6 base pts)
study_type_values.clinical_strain: Probiotic strain clinical evidence (4 base pts)
study_type_values.in_vitro: In vitro/cell studies only (1 base pt)

_metadata._scoring_formula: score = study_type_base_points × evidence_level_multiplier (computed live by scorer, not stored)

_scoring_fields_used.study_type: Determines base points: systematic_review_meta=6, rct_multiple=5, rct_single=4, clinical_strain=4, observational=2, animal_study=2, in_vitro=1
_scoring_fields_used.evidence_level: Determines multiplier: product-human=1.0, branded-rct=0.8, ingredient-human=0.65, strain-clinical=0.6, preclinical=0.3
_scoring_fields_used.score_contribution: Metadata label (tier_1/tier_2/tier_3) reflecting computed score bracket. Not used by scorer.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Multiple human placebo-controlled trials for arthritis, inflammation, bioavailability.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Belcaro 2010 (n=50) WOMAC -58%, CRP 168->11 mg/L in OA, Panminerva Med; Antiga 2015 (n=63) RCT psoriasis IL-22 reduction. 35+ human trials on Meriva phytosome.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Published trials for stress/anxiety, libido, muscle, memory; full-spectrum root extract.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Chandrasekhar 2012 (n=64) RCT cortisol -28% vs placebo (p=0.0006), Indian J Psychol Med; Langade 2019 (n=80) RCT sleep onset latency improvement. 50+ published studies.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Standardized root+leaf extract (Natreon); 7+ published RCTs for stress, cortisol reduction, sleep quality, cognition, and strength. JANA 2008 study is the landmark trial.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Auddy 2008 (n=98) RCT 3-dose cortisol reduction, 62% stress decrease vs placebo, JANA; Raut 2012 (n=18) dose-escalation safety. 7+ published RCTs.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Curcumin with turmeric oil; multiple RCTs in inflammation, depression, joint pain.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Shep 2019 (n=139) RCT BCM-95 500mg TID matched diclofenac in knee OA, Trials; Sanmukhani 2014 (n=60) RCT major depression response comparable to fluoxetine, Phytother Res.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Nanoparticle curcumin; high absorption. Trials on arthritis, inflammation, cognition.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Small 2018 (n=40) 18-month RCT memory +28%, amyloid/tau decreased on PET, Am J Geriatr Psychiatry; Nakagawa 2014 (n=50) RCT knee OA pain reduction vs placebo.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: UK NHS-prescribed 24-nutrient multivitamin for bariatric and malnutrition patients. No product-specific RCTs; evidence base is the individual component nutrients. Clinical use is based on nutritional completeness, not trial-demonstrated superiority.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: No Forceval-specific RCTs found. UK NHS-prescribed 24-nutrient multivitamin for bariatric/malnutrition patients. Evidence based on component nutrients, not product-specific trials.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Life Extension retail product using BCM-95 (CurcuGreen). Clinical evidence derives from BCM-95 ingredient studies, not product-specific trials. Antony 2008 crossover showed 6.93x higher bioavailability vs standard curcumin.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Uses BCM-95 (CurcuGreen) ingredient. Antony 2008 (n=11) PK crossover showed 6.93x higher AUC vs standard curcumin. No trials specific to Life Extension product; relies on BCM-95 data.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Multiple clinical studies support use for circulation, skin, ADHD, blood pressure.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Belcaro 2008 (n=156) RCT WOMAC -56% in knee OA; meta-analysis of 24 RCTs (n=1594) cardiometabolic endpoints. 39 double-blind placebo-controlled studies total on Pycnogenol.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Backed for antibiotic-associated diarrhea, gut infections, IBS.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: S. boulardii CNCM I-745 meta-analysis pediatric diarrhea (6 RCTs, duration -1.6 days); Villar-Garcia 2015 (n=44) RCT HIV gut barrier. 130+ clinical studies on the strain.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Natural astaxanthin from H. pluvialis; multiple clinical studies for eye strain, skin photoprotection, and exercise recovery.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Tominaga 2012 (n=36) RCT wrinkle/elasticity improvement at 6mg/day for 6 weeks; majority of published astaxanthin RCTs use AstaReal source material. 50%+ of astaxanthin clinical evidence.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: One small RCT (n=28) showed reduced dietary endotoxemia; additional evidence is in-vitro (SHIME model) and observational. Spore-based probiotic (Bacillus strains). Evidence base is limited relative to marketing claims.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: McFarlin 2017 (n=28) 30-day RCT reduced post-prandial endotoxemia and triglycerides, World J Gastrointest Pathophysiol. Only 1 small human RCT; additional evidence is in-vitro (SHIME model).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Major clinical trials in heart failure, statin users; preferred bioactive CoQ10.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Sabbatinelli 2020 (n=51) RCT 37% FMD improvement in dyslipidemia at 100mg/day, Nutrients; Hosoe 2007 safety/PK to 300mg. 85+ clinical studies on Kaneka ubiquinol.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Buffered vitamin C; clinical evidence for improved bioavailability.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Moyad 2008 (n=15) crossover RCT higher leukocyte vitamin C at 24h vs ascorbic acid; Van Straten 2002 (n=168) RCT fewer colds. Limited evidence for clinical superiority over standard vitamin C.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: No published clinical trials found for ZyloFresh branded extract. Generic alfalfa (Medicago sativa) has limited RCT evidence for lipid reduction. Brand evidence appears manufacturer-only and unverifiable in peer-reviewed literature.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: No published clinical trials found for ZyloFresh branded extract. Generic alfalfa has limited RCT evidence (Li 2023) for lipid reduction only.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: German-manufactured creatine with 99.95% purity. Extensive research in strength, power, cognitive function.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Creapure (Alzchem) used in multiple published RCTs. Forbes 2023 (n=120) RCT cognitive function; 99.95% purity specification. Extensive generic creatine monohydrate RCT base applies.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Patented amino acid chelated minerals with superior bioavailability studies.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Scagliusi 2021 Mg bisglycinate chelate RCT leg cramps -79% vs -32% placebo (p=0.007); Zn bisglycinate +43% absorption vs gluconate in crossover study. 70+ human trials on chelated minerals.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Fourth-generation glucosamine salt of 5-MTHF (Gnosis). PK studies show 1.8-3.1x higher bioavailability vs folic acid; homocysteine reduction demonstrated. EFSA-reviewed. Most evidence is PK/biomarker studies, not disease-endpoint RCTs.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Mazza 2016 homocysteine reduction 21.5->10.0 umol/L with 400ug; PK crossover 1.8x higher Cmax vs Ca-5-MTHF. EFSA-reviewed. Most evidence is PK/biomarker, not disease-endpoint RCTs.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: European-approved 2mg prolonged-release melatonin with extensive sleep studies.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Lemoine 2007 (n=170) RCT 2mg PR-melatonin sleep quality +26% vs 15% placebo (p=0.014); Wade 2010 (n=930) long-term RCT no rebound/withdrawal. EMA-approved prescription drug.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Brand identity unverified — Glanbia's lactoferrin brand is 'Bioferrin', not 'OptiFerrin'. Lactoferrin generically has RCT support for iron status (Zhao 2022 meta-analysis) and neonatal infection prevention. Verify brand name against supplier.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Brand identity uncertain (Glanbia markets 'Bioferrin'). Lactoferrin meta-analysis Zhao 2022 shows ferritin increase vs ferrous sulfate. No published RCTs found for 'OptiFerrin' specifically.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Pharmaceutical-grade L-carnitine with studies in exercise performance, heart health.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Volek 2002 RCT 2g/day reduced muscle soreness and purine metabolites; Ho 2010 (n=80) RCT 5-week recovery improvement, Nutrients. 9 peer-reviewed articles from UConn partnership 2001-2010.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Organic kelp extract with natural iodine; studies in thyroid function.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Clark et al. (2003) double-blind RCT (n=36): TSH increased significantly in kelp groups with dose-dependent urinary iodine rises (Endocr Pract 9:363). No branded sea kelp product has proprietary RCTs; evidence is for generic kelp/seaweed iodine supplementation only.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Clinical studies in memory, cognitive function, stress response, exercise recovery.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Vakhapova et al. (2010) double-blind RCT (n=157): soy-derived PS (Sharp-PS) 100 mg/d for 15 wk improved memory and learning in elderly with complaints (J Clin Interv Aging 5:313). Kato-Kataoka et al. (2010) RCT (n=78): soy-PS 100 mg/d for 6 mo improved memory in elderly with MCI (J Clin Biochem Nutr 47:246).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Australian pharmaceutical-grade extract; clinical studies in liver function.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: No MediHerb-brand-specific RCTs identified. Generic silymarin: Fried et al. (2012) multi-center RCT (n=154): silymarin 420-700 mg TID for 24 wk in HCV showed no ALT reduction (JAMA 308:274). Wah Kheong et al. (2017) RCT (n=99): 700 mg TID for 48 wk improved fibrosis/steatosis in NASH (Clin Gastroenterol Hepatol 15:1940).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Unique mitochondrial-targeted antioxidant. Human RCT in vascular, cellular stress.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Rossman et al. (2018) crossover RCT (n=20, 60-79 yrs): MitoQ 20 mg/d for 6 wk improved brachial FMD by ~42% and reduced aortic stiffness vs placebo (Hypertension 71:1056). Larger confirmatory RCTs ongoing (NCT04851288).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Memory, cognition, neuroprotection in elderly and stroke patients.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Nakazaki et al. (2021) RCT (n=100, 50-85 yrs): Cognizin 500 mg/d for 12 wk improved episodic memory (Paired Associate test) and composite memory vs placebo (J Nutr 151:2153). McGlade et al. (2012) RCT (n=60 women): 250-500 mg/d for 28 d improved attentional performance (Food Nutr Sci 3:769).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Green tea amino acid, calming effect; validated anxiolytic in studies.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Hidese et al. (2019) crossover RCT (n=30): Suntheanine 200 mg/d for 4 wk reduced stress symptoms (depression, anxiety, sleep) and improved verbal fluency vs placebo (Nutrients 11:2362). Lyon et al. (2011) RCT (n=98 boys, 8-12 yrs, ADHD): 400 mg/d for 6 wk improved sleep quality and reduced sleep onset latency (Altern Med Rev 16:348).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Spore-forming probiotic with clinical studies in digestive health and IBS.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Majeed et al. (2016) RCT (n=36, IBS-D): LactoSpore B. coagulans MTCC 5856 2B CFU/d for 90 d significantly reduced bloating, pain, diarrhea (Nutr J 15:21). Majeed et al. (2018) RCT (n=40, MDD+IBS): 2B CFU/d for 90 d improved depression (HAMD), IBS-QOL, and myeloperoxidase vs placebo (Food Nutr Res 62:1218).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Branded lutein/zeaxanthin blend with clinical eye health studies.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: AREDS2 (2013, n=4,203): 10 mg lutein + 2 mg OPTISHARP zeaxanthin reduced AMD progression by 26% in lowest dietary intake quintile (JAMA 309:2005). Hammond et al. (2014) RCT (n=115): same dose for 12 mo improved macular pigment density, photostress recovery, and glare disability (IOVS 55:8583).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Baker's yeast beta-glucan with multiple immune function RCTs.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Auinger et al. (2013) RCT (n=162): Wellmune 250 mg/d for 16 wk reduced URTI symptom days and increased salivary IgA vs placebo (Eur J Nutr 52:1913). Carpenter et al. (2013) RCT (n=182 marathon runners): 250 mg/d for 4 wk post-race showed 37% fewer cold/flu symptom days (J Diet Suppl 10:171).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Patented black pepper extract proven to enhance absorption of nutrients and herbs.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Shoba et al. (1998) crossover (n=8): BioPerine 20 mg co-administered with curcumin 2 g increased curcumin AUC by 2000% via glucuronidation inhibition (Planta Med 64:353). Badmaev et al. (2000) crossover: 5 mg/d enhanced beta-carotene (~60%) and CoQ10 (~30%) absorption. Note: 2000% claim questioned by Fanca-Berthon 2021 replication.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Tripeptide form of glutathione with clinical studies showing increased blood glutathione levels.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Richie et al. (2015) RCT (n=54, 6 mo): Setria GSH 250/1000 mg/d increased blood GSH 30-35%, buccal cell GSH up to 260%; 1000 mg doubled NK cell cytotoxicity vs placebo (Eur J Nutr 54:251). Weschawalit et al. (2017) RCT (n=60 women): 500 mg/d oral GSH for 4 wk reduced melanin index.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Distilled MSM with clinical studies in joint health and exercise recovery.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Kim et al. (2006) RCT (n=50, knee OA): OptiMSM 6 g/d for 12 wk improved WOMAC pain and physical function vs placebo (Osteoarthritis Cartilage 14:286). Crawford et al. (2023) RCT (n=88): 2 g/d for 12 wk improved KOOS quality of life subscale vs placebo (Nutrients 15:2995).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Multi-mineral complex from marine algae with bone and joint health studies.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Frestedt et al. (2008) RCT (n=70, knee OA): Aquamin 2400 mg/d for 12 wk reduced NSAID use with trend toward improved WOMAC vs placebo (Nutr J 8:7). Aquamin provides 74 trace minerals from Lithothamnion red algae; larger confirmatory OA trials needed.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Patented solid lipid formulation; multiple human studies on brain health, mood, and superior absorption compared to standard curcumin.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Cox et al. (2015) RCT (n=60, 60-85 yrs): Longvida 400 mg/d for 4 wk improved working memory, sustained attention, reduced fatigue vs placebo (J Psychopharmacol 29:642). Cox et al. (2020) RCT (n=80, 50-80 yrs): 12 wk confirmed working memory and fatigue benefits. Solid Lipid Curcumin Particle tech provides 65x higher free curcumin bioavailability.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Standardized to 95% curcuminoids; over 80 clinical trials on conditions like arthritis, metabolic syndrome, and liver health.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Chandran & Goel (2012) RCT (n=45, RA): C3 Complex 500 mg/d for 8 wk outperformed diclofenac on DAS28 (p<0.05) with fewer adverse effects (Phytother Res 26:1719). Amalraj et al. (2016) RCT (n=36, OA): C3 Complex 500 mg + BioPerine for 12 wk improved pain VAS and WOMAC (J Med Food 20:1022). Claims 80+ published studies.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Patented low-dose collagen; RCTs show efficacy in osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis, better than glucosamine/chondroitin combos.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Lugo et al. (2016) RCT (n=191, knee OA, 180 d): UC-II 40 mg/d improved WOMAC total, pain, function vs placebo and vs glucosamine+chondroitin (Int J Med Sci 13:169). Lugo et al. (2013) RCT (n=55): 40 mg/d for 120 d improved knee extension ROM in healthy volunteers (Int J Clin Pharmacol Res 33:135).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Matrix of collagen II, chondroitin, and HA; human trials for skin aging and joint support.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Schauss et al. (2012) RCT (n=80, OA): BioCell 2 g/d for 70 d reduced VAS pain and WOMAC; 40% achieved ≥30% pain improvement vs 3% placebo (J Agric Food Chem 60:4096). Schwartz & Park (2012) open-label (n=26): 1 g/d for 12 wk reduced wrinkles and skin dryness.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Patented NR precursor; multiple human RCTs on NAD+ boosting, safety, and benefits for aging and muscle.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Martens et al. (2018) crossover RCT (n=24, 55-79 yrs): Niagen NR 1000 mg/d for 6 wk raised whole-blood NAD+ ~60%, trends toward reduced SBP (-9 mmHg) and aortic stiffness (Nat Commun 9:1286). Dollerup et al. (2018) RCT (n=40 obese men): NR 2000 mg/d for 12 wk confirmed NAD+ increase but no insulin sensitivity change (Am J Clin Nutr 108:343).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Natural MK-7 form; clinical trials on osteoporosis prevention and vascular health.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Knapen et al. (2015) landmark 3-year RCT (n=244 postmenopausal women): MenaQ7 180 mcg/d significantly improved arterial stiffness and carotid distensibility — first evidence K2 reverses vascular calcification (Thromb Haemost 113:1135). Knapen et al. (2013) same cohort: improved osteocalcin carboxylation and reduced femoral neck BMD decline (Osteoporos Int 24:2499).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Standardized to Lepticrosalides; RCTs in adolescents and adults for mood and sleep disorders.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Kell et al. (2017) RCT (n=128): affron 28 mg/d for 4 wk improved PANAS mood, DASS-21 depression/anxiety vs placebo (Complement Ther Med 33:58). Lopresti et al. (2025) largest saffron mood trial RCT (n=202): 28 mg/d for 12 wk — 53% DASS-21 depression reduction vs placebo. Standardized to 3.5% Lepticrosalides.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Proprietary blend; clinical studies on stress reduction and weight control in stressed individuals.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Talbott et al. (2013) RCT (n=56): Relora 250 mg TID for 4 wk lowered salivary cortisol 18%, improved mood (-42% anger, -31% fatigue, +18% vigor) vs placebo (JISSN 10:37). Kalman et al. (2008) RCT (n=40 women): 250 mg TID for 6 wk reduced perceived anxiety (Nutr J 7:11).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Standardized extract; extensive trials for age-related cognitive decline and peripheral circulation.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Ihl et al. (2011) RCT (n=410, dementia): EGb 761 240 mg/d for 24 wk improved SKT cognition, NPI, ADL, and quality of life vs placebo (J Psychiatr Res 45:1574). Most extensively studied ginkgo extract with 7+ major RCTs in 2,600+ dementia patients and confirming meta-analyses.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Patented urolithin A; human trials on mitochondrial health and physical performance in aging.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Singh et al. (2022) ATLAS trial RCT (n=88, 40-64 yrs): Mitopure 1000 mg/d for 4 mo improved hamstring strength ~12%, peak VO2, and reduced CRP indicating improved mitochondrial function (Cell Rep Med 3:100633). Andreux et al. (2019) first-in-human RCT (n=60): 4 wk confirmed safety and upregulated mitochondrial biomarkers in muscle biopsies (Nat Metab 1:595).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Well-studied for high-intensity exercise; increases carnosine levels in muscles.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Saunders et al. (2017) meta-analysis (40 studies, n=1,461): overall ES 0.18 (95% CI 0.08-0.28); exercise capacity ES 0.50 for 0.5-10 min efforts. Hobson et al. (2012) meta-analysis (15 studies): median effect d=0.18, benefit greatest for 1-4 min tasks.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human trials support use for endurance, blood pressure, and erectile function.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Rhim et al. (2020) meta-analysis (13 RCTs, n=198): L-citrulline reduced RPE and improved high-intensity exercise performance. Allerton et al. (2018) systematic review (12 studies): citrulline supplementation ≥6 g/d improved endurance and strength performance. Figueroa et al. MAs confirm SBP/DBP reduction.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Strong 2024-2025 systematic review evidence from multiple meta-analyses supports hydrolyzed collagen peptides for skin (hydration, elasticity, wrinkles) and joint health. Low molecular weight peptides (<6 kDa) offer superior absorption. Benefits typically after 90 days.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: 2024-2025 systematic review of 26 RCTs (n=1,721): hydrolyzed collagen significantly improved skin hydration (Z=4.94, P<0.00001) and elasticity (Z=4.49, P<0.00001). 2025 review of type I collagen (n=1,652, studies 2011-2024) showed positive outcomes for joints.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Oral supplementation shown in trials to improve knee osteoarthritis and skin health.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Oe et al. (2017) meta-analysis (7 RCTs): oral HA 120-240 mg/d improved skin hydration and reduced wrinkle depth after 2-8 wk. Systematic review of 11 OA studies: 9/11 showed improved pain and WOMAC scores.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Strong evidence from trials for hypertension and lipid profile improvement.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Ried (2020) meta-analysis (12 RCTs, n=553): garlic reduced SBP -8.3 mmHg, DBP -5.5 mmHg in hypertensives; lowered TC 0.28 mmol/L. Bahadoran et al. (2023) meta-analysis (22 RCTs): significantly reduced TC, LDL-C, triglycerides, fasting glucose in metabolic syndrome.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human studies confirm roles in preventing arterial stiffness and osteoporosis.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Ma et al. (2022) meta-analysis (16 RCTs): K2 increased lumbar spine BMD (p=0.028 females), increased carboxylated osteocalcin (p=0.004). Knapen et al. (2015) 3-year RCT (n=244 postmenopausal women): MK-7 180 mcg/d inhibited arterial stiffening and improved vascular elasticity.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Trials support use in thyroid disorders and as an antioxidant.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Kuria et al. (2023) umbrella review (76 meta-analyses, 96 outcomes): selenium decreased risk of digestive cancers, all-cause mortality, depression; improved sperm quality and thyroid autoimmunity. Evidence does not support supplementing beyond RDI for immune function.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human trials for depression, fibromyalgia, and weight loss via serotonin modulation.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Maffei (2020) systematic review & meta-analysis: 5-HTP depression remission rate 0.65 (95% CI 0.55-0.78), large effect (Hedges' g=1.11). Shaw et al. (2002) Cochrane-style analysis: tryptophan/5-HTP better than placebo (Peto OR 4.1); limited by small samples and publication bias.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Comparable to prescription antidepressants in trials; also effective for osteoarthritis.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Li et al. (2024) meta-analysis (23 trials, n=2,183): SAMe improved HDRS by ~6 points; adjunctive SAMe with antidepressants showed greater efficacy. Soeken et al. (2002) meta-analysis (4 OA trials, n=656): SAMe as effective as NSAIDs with fewer adverse effects (ES 0.31).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Extensively studied for cognitive enhancement, fat burning, and athletic performance.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Guest et al. (2021) ISSN position stand (400+ studies): 3-6 mg/kg caffeine improves endurance (+2.9% mean power), strength, sprint, and cognitive vigilance. Machado et al. (2021) meta-analysis (13 RCTs, n=244): improved attention (ES 0.38) and accuracy in sports tasks.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Extensively studied for blood sugar control, lipid management, weight management.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Zamani et al. (2024) meta-analysis (50 RCTs, n=4,150): berberine reduced FBG -7.74 mg/dL, HbA1c -0.45%, TC -20.64 mg/dL, LDL -13.4 mg/dL vs placebo. Dong et al. (2012) meta-analysis (14 RCTs, n=1,068): significantly reduced FBG, HbA1c, TG, TC in T2DM.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Cocoa flavanol with studies in muscle growth, cardiovascular health, exercise performance.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Hooper et al. (2012) Cochrane meta-analysis (20 RCTs, n=856): chocolate/cocoa improved FMD +1.45% and reduced DBP; doses >50 mg epicatechin/d showed greater BP effects. Mastroiacovo et al. (2015) RCT (n=90, elderly): cocoa flavanols improved cognitive performance dose-dependently.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Standardized rhodiola extract with consistent clinical results in fatigue and stress.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Wang et al. (2025) meta-analysis (26 RCTs): Rhodiola rosea improved VO2 max, time to exhaustion, antioxidant capacity; reduced CK and lactate. Olsson et al. (2009) RCT (n=60): SHR-5 extract (3% salidroside) reduced stress-related fatigue and improved cognition over 28 d.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: 2024-2025 evidence supports magnesium bisglycinate for sleep quality and anxiety. August 2025 RCT confirms modest but significant improvements with excellent tolerability. Superior GI tolerance vs magnesium oxide.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: August 2025 RCT (n=155) showed magnesium bisglycinate (250mg Mg + 1523mg glycine daily) significantly reduced Insomnia Severity Index with effect size d=0.2. 2024 systematic review of 15 studies found benefits for anxiety and sleep.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Extensive systematic review evidence supports NAC as powerful antioxidant and glutathione precursor. Well-established for respiratory health (mucolytic, COPD), liver protection, and emerging psychiatric applications. Modulates glutamate pathways.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Multiple systematic reviews support NAC for glutathione enhancement, liver support, and respiratory health. Strong evidence for COPD, chronic bronchitis, acetaminophen overdose. RCTs demonstrate benefits for psychiatric conditions including bipolar, depression, OCD, and addiction.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human studies show anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects; often studied in combination with zinc or vitamin C.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Serban et al. (2016) meta-analysis (7 RCTs, n=587): quercetin reduced SBP -3.04 mmHg and DBP -2.63 mmHg; effects greater at >500 mg/d. Abdollahi et al. (2024) umbrella review: quercetin reduced TC, LDL-C, CRP across multiple meta-analyses.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Shown to reduce stress and improve focus in healthy adults; synergistic with caffeine.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Williams et al. (2020) systematic review (9 RCTs, n=270): L-theanine 200 mg reduced subjective stress and anxiety under acute stress. Kasprzak-Drozd et al. (2024) meta-analysis (10 RCTs): 50-500 mg did not significantly improve simple reaction time; higher doses improved visual processing speed.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Improves memory and attention in healthy adults and elderly; standardized extracts used in trials.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Kongkeaw et al. (2014) meta-analysis (9 RCTs, n=518, ≥12 wk): Bacopa monnieri 300-450 mg/d significantly improved speed of attention (p<0.001) and cognitive processing. Pase et al. (2012) systematic review (6 RCTs): improved 9/17 memory free recall tests.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Strong 2024 evidence supports CoQ10 (ubiquinone preferred over ubiquinol) for heart failure with significant mortality and hospitalization reductions. Improves ATP production and mitochondrial function. Effective for statin-induced myopathy.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: October 2024 BMC meta-analysis of 33 RCTs: CoQ10 reduced all-cause mortality (RR=0.64, P=0.002) and HF hospitalization (RR=0.50, P<0.00001). July 2024 review found increased ATP (SMD 0.82, P<0.001) and mitochondrial capacity (SMD 0.75, P<0.001).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Extensive systematic review evidence supports omega-3 for cardiovascular health, triglyceride reduction, and mood. High-dose EPA particularly effective for CV risk reduction. Quality and formulation affect bioavailability.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Hu et al. (2019) JAMA Cardiology meta-analysis (13 RCTs, n=127,477): marine omega-3 supplementation reduced CHD death (RR 0.92), MI (RR 0.87), and total CHD events (RR 0.93). Abdelhamid et al. (2020) Cochrane (86 RCTs): long-chain omega-3 slightly reduced CHD mortality and events.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Extensive evidence from systematic reviews and meta-analyses (2024-2025) supports vitamin D3 for bone health, immune function, and mood. 2024 Endocrine Society guidelines recommend empiric supplementation for specific populations.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Bolland et al. (2018) Lancet meta-analysis (81 RCTs, n=53,537): vitamin D did not prevent fractures or falls or improve BMD. Martineau et al. (2017) Cochrane (25 RCTs, n=11,321): vitamin D reduced acute respiratory infections (OR 0.88); benefit greatest in deficient individuals.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Strong 2024 evidence shows zinc lozenges (>75mg/day elemental zinc) substantially reduce cold duration when started within 24 hours. Zinc picolinate form shows good bioavailability and tolerance.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: 2024 analysis showed zinc lozenges (>75mg/day) shortened common cold duration by 33-37% (P<10^-7). Zinc acetate and gluconate lozenges both effective. Meta-analysis showed 2.25 days reduction. Start within 24 hours of onset.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human studies support macular pigment density and visual performance improvements.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Ma et al. (2012) meta-analysis (6 cohorts, n=185,000+): highest lutein/zeaxanthin intake reduced AMD risk 26% (RR 0.74). Nouchi et al. (2021) meta-analysis (5 RCTs): lutein improved visual episodic memory and processing speed; MPOD increased 0.11 with ≥20 mg/d.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Moderate evidence from case series and open-label trials support biotin for hair and nail health, particularly in deficiency states. Limited placebo-controlled RCT evidence. Vitamin B7 essential for fatty acid synthesis. High doses (>10mg) may interfere with lab tests.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Used in trials for hair loss and brittle nails with moderate evidence in deficiency states. Limited high-quality RCT evidence in non-deficient individuals. Some open-label studies show improvements in hair and nail quality.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Strong systematic review evidence supports melatonin for sleep onset latency, jet lag, and circadian rhythm disorders. Multiple meta-analyses confirm dose-dependent effects. Particularly effective for jet lag, shift work, delayed sleep phase. Generally safe short-term.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Multiple systematic reviews and meta-analyses confirm melatonin effectiveness for sleep onset, jet lag, and circadian rhythm disorders. Strong evidence for shift work disorder and delayed sleep phase syndrome. Dose-dependent: 0.5-5mg typically effective. Prolonged-release (Circadin) benefits insomnia in adults 55+.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Improves mental performance, immune function, and reduces fatigue in human studies.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Liu et al. (2024) meta-analysis (15 RCTs, n=671): Panax ginseng improved memory (SMD 0.19, p<0.05); high-dose SMD 0.33. Bach et al. (2016) meta-analysis (8 RCTs): ginseng reduced fatigue scores and improved heart rate recovery vs placebo.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Shown to reduce stress-related fatigue and improve mood in multiple clinical trials.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Wang et al. (2025) meta-analysis (26 RCTs): Rhodiola rosea improved VO2 max, time to exhaustion, antioxidant capacity, reduced lactate and CK. Ishaque et al. (2012) systematic review (11 trials): evidence suggests benefit for physical performance and mental fatigue.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: 2024-2025 systematic reviews confirm B12 essential for nerve and blood cell health. Clear benefits in deficiency and subclinical deficiency. Methylcobalamin and adenosylcobalamin are active forms.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Wang et al. (2018) systematic review (18 observational, n=34,000+): higher B12 associated with lower cognitive decline. Bala et al. (2021) meta-analysis: B12+folate supplementation improved MMSE in elderly with/without dementia. Largest evidence base is for deficiency correction.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: 2024-2025 systematic reviews strongly support Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium for gut health and immune function. Improved intestinal barrier, microbiota modulation, SCFA production. Strain-specific effects: L. acidophilus and B. lactis most successful.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: 2024-2025 systematic reviews show probiotics significantly boost Bifidobacterium (SMD 1.85, P=0.01) and Lactobacillus (SMD 2.22, P=0.02). 2024 study showed Lactobacillus-based probiotics decolonized pathogens (62% vs 53%, P=0.04). Benefits for IBS with B. breve, B. longum, L. acidophilus.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Strong 2024-2025 systematic review evidence supports curcumin for anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Meta-analyses confirm inflammatory marker reductions. Moderate quality evidence for OA pain relief. Bioavailability major limitation; enhanced formulations show >100-fold improvement.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: 2024-2025 systematic reviews/meta-analyses (66 RCTs): curcumin significantly reduces CRP (WMD -0.58 mg/L), TNF-α (WMD -3.48 pg/mL), IL-6 (WMD -1.31 pg/mL). Recent meta-analysis in prediabetes/T2D found reductions in inflammatory markers. Greater benefits ≥1g/day.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: 2024-2025 systematic reviews confirm vitamin C's role in immune function, antioxidant defense, and collagen synthesis. Enhanced forms (calcium ascorbate, liposomal) show improved tolerance. Shortens cold duration.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: 2025 systematic review in Nutrients found calcium ascorbate EC superior to ascorbic acid for tolerability and leukocyte concentrations. 2024 review confirms vitamin C reduces cold duration, greater effects on severe symptoms.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human trials show benefits for nausea, joint pain, and anti-inflammatory effects.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Jalali et al. (2020) meta-analysis (20 articles, 25 study arms): ginger reduced CRP -0.84 mg/L, hs-CRP, and TNF-a. Viljoen et al. (2014) systematic review (12 RCTs, n=1,278): 1-1.5 g/d reduced nausea in pregnancy 4-fold; also effective for chemotherapy-induced vomiting.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Strong 2024-2025 systematic review evidence supports ashwagandha for stress and anxiety reduction. Consistently reduces cortisol through HPA axis modulation. Multiple meta-analyses confirm anxiety scale benefits. Generic extract effective at 250-600mg daily.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: January 2025 systematic review: ashwagandha significantly reduced cortisol (-1.16 μg/dL, P<0.001). 2024 meta-analysis of 9 RCTs (n=558): significant improvements in PSS (MD=-4.72), Hamilton Anxiety (MD=-2.19), cortisol (MD=-2.58). Dose 250-600mg/day for 4-13 weeks.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human studies show metabolic benefits, antioxidant effects, and fat oxidation enhancement.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Asbaghi et al. (2023) GRADE meta-analysis (59 RCTs, n=3,802): green tea extract reduced body mass, BMI, body fat %, oxidative stress; increased adiponectin. Hursel et al. (2009) meta-analysis (11 RCTs): catechin-caffeine mixtures increased 24-h EE by 4.7% and fat oxidation 16%.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Clinical studies support liver protection and enzyme normalization.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Kalopitas et al. (2024) meta-analysis (26 RCTs, n=2,375 NAFLD/NASH): silymarin reduced ALT, AST, TC, TG, LDL-C, fasting insulin; improved steatosis on histology. Earlier Cochrane reviews found no benefit in viral hepatitis/cirrhosis mortality.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human trials show cognitive improvements and nerve growth factor stimulation in elderly with mild cognitive impairment.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Docherty et al. (2023) systematic review (5 RCTs + 15 lab studies): H. erinaceus improved MMSE +1.17 pts; hericenones/erinacines stimulate NGF/BDNF. Mori et al. (2009) RCT (n=30 elderly, MCI): 3 g/d for 16 wk improved cognitive function vs placebo; benefits reversed 4 wk post-cessation.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Clinical trials demonstrate improved sleep quality and reduced sleep onset time through thermoregulatory effects.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Bannai et al. (2012) crossover RCT (n=11): 3 g glycine before bed improved subjective sleep quality, reduced latency via PSG, decreased next-day fatigue. Razak et al. (2024) systematic review: glycine improved sleep via NMDA receptor-mediated SCN hypothermia; limited by small samples.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Natural source of melatonin with human studies showing improved sleep duration and quality.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Hill et al. (2021) meta-analysis (14 RCTs): tart cherry had moderate benefit on muscular strength recovery (ES 0.34) and small effect reducing soreness. Hao et al. (2023) meta-analysis (8 studies, n=332): significantly decreased CRP. 5/6 sleep studies showed improved total sleep time.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Multiple RCTs and meta-analyses demonstrate elderberry (Sambucus nigra) reduces cold and flu duration/severity. 2021 review confirms safety with no cytokine storm concerns. Antiviral effects via immune stimulation. Start at symptom onset. Added plain "elderberry" alias for exact-match coverage.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: 2021 BMC systematic review of 5 RCTs (n=180) found elderberry reduces cold/flu duration and severity with large effect sizes. 2019 meta-analysis showed substantial reduction in upper respiratory symptoms. May be comparable to oseltamivir with fewer side effects.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human studies show blood glucose reduction and testosterone support in men.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Mansoori et al. (2020) meta-analysis (4 RCTs, n=449): fenugreek increased total testosterone (SMD 0.32); saponins modulate aromatase/5-alpha reductase. Neelakantan et al. (2014) meta-analysis (10 RCTs): reduced FBG -0.96 mmol/L, HbA1c; improved lipid profiles.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Traditional medicine with human studies supporting blood glucose management.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Peter et al. (2019) Frontiers in Nutrition systematic review: M. charantia showed mixed glycemic benefits across heterogeneous preparations. Yin et al. (2023) SR (9 RCTs): no significant overall benefit on glucose or CV risk factors; small samples and heterogeneous formulations limit conclusions.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Multiple RCTs for diabetic neuropathy, blood sugar control, and antioxidant effects.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Agathos et al. (2018) meta-analysis (24 RCTs): ALA reduced FBG, fasting insulin, HOMA-IR, HbA1c, and improved lipid profile in metabolic diseases. Ziegler et al. (2006) SYDNEY 2 trial RCT (n=181): ALA 600 mg/d for 5 wk significantly improved neuropathic symptoms vs placebo (Diabetes Care 29:2365).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: FDA-approved heart health claim; extensive evidence for cholesterol reduction.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Abumweis et al. (2008) meta-analysis (59 RCTs): plant sterols/stanols reduced LDL-C 0.31 mmol/L (8.8%); effect continued up to 3 g/d (~12% reduction). Scholle et al. (2016) meta-analysis: additionally lowered TC and LDL-C in statin-treated patients beyond monotherapy.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Contains natural statins; multiple RCTs for cholesterol management.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Li et al. (2022) meta-analysis (15 RCTs): RYR reduced TC 0.91 mmol/L and LDL-C 0.73 mmol/L vs placebo; comparable to low-dose statin. Active monacolin K is structurally identical to lovastatin; 3-10 mg/d achieves 15-25% LDL reduction within 6-8 wk.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Multiple RCTs for osteoarthritis; prescription form available in Europe.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Veronese et al. (2020) umbrella review (11 SRs, 37 RCTs): Rx-grade glucosamine sulfate 1500 mg/d is safe and reduces joint space narrowing. Zhu et al. (2018) meta-analysis (20 RCTs): reduced OA pain (SMD -0.34); crystalline glucosamine sulfate more effective than HCl form.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Clinical evidence for joint health; often combined with glucosamine.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Singh et al. (2015) Cochrane meta-analysis (43 RCTs, n=9,110): chondroitin 800-1200 mg/d reduced OA pain (SMD -0.63) and improved function (SMD -0.82). Kahan et al. (2009) structural meta-analysis (3 RCTs, 2-yr): small but significant structure-modifying effect on joint space narrowing.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Multiple RCTs for urinary tract health and UTI prevention.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Williams et al. (2023) Cochrane (50 RCTs, n=8,857): cranberry reduced UTI risk 30% (RR 0.70, moderate certainty); women with recurrent UTIs and children benefited most. PACs inhibit P-fimbriated E. coli adhesion to urothelial cells; ≥36 mg PACs/d recommended.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Multiple RCTs showing blood glucose improvements in type 2 diabetes.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Namazi et al. (2023) meta-analysis (24 RCTs): cinnamon reduced FBG -17.08 mg/dL, HOMA-IR, HbA1c in T2DM; greater benefit at >1.5 g/d. Allen et al. (2013) meta-analysis (10 RCTs, n=543): reduced FBG 3-5%, TC, LDL-C, TG; increased HDL-C.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Clinical evidence for digestive support and pancreatic insufficiency.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Ianiro et al. (2016) review: enzyme supplementation (lipase, protease, amylase) improved fat/protein absorption in exocrine pancreatic insufficiency. Majeed et al. (2023) RCT: multi-enzyme blend reduced functional dyspepsia symptoms (bloating, fullness, pain) vs placebo over 60 d. No general-population meta-analysis exists.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Prebiotic fiber with RCTs for gut microbiome and mineral absorption.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Vandeputte et al. (2017) systematic review (9 RCTs): inulin 5-20 g/d consistently increased Bifidobacterium, Lactobacillus, and F. prausnitzii. Bonsu & Johnson (2021) systematic review: inulin-type fructans improved barrier function, laxation, Ca/Mg absorption, insulin sensitivity, and TG across multiple RCTs.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: 2023-2025 systematic review evidence supports ferrous bisglycinate as superior chelated iron with better absorption and GI tolerance than ferrous sulfate. Meta-analysis confirms hemoglobin and ferritin increases with fewer adverse events, particularly in pregnancy.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: 2023 systematic review/meta-analysis: ferrous bisglycinate shows benefit over other iron supplements for hemoglobin and reducing GI adverse events in pregnant women. 2025 study: bisglycinate reversed iron deficiency in 14 days without GI inflammation (unlike ferrous sulfate).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: 2024-2025 evidence shows 5-MTHF is bioavailable active folate form avoiding MTHFR gene concerns and reducing unmetabolized folic acid. Multiple RCTs confirm equivalence to folic acid with lower UMFA. Beneficial for MTHFR polymorphisms. Growing support for prenatal use.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: January 2024 RCT (British Journal of Nutrition): 5-MTHF as effective as folic acid for maternal folate while reducing unmetabolized folic acid by ~50%. 2024 reviews support for pregnancy. Both low and high maternal folate associated with increased CHD risk (U-shaped curve).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Multiple RCTs for sleep improvement; traditional use validated.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Shinjyo et al. (2020) meta-analysis (60 studies, n=6,894): valerian improved subjective sleep quality (10 studies, n=1,065) and reduced anxiety (8 studies, n=535). Bent et al. (2006) meta-analysis (16 RCTs, n=1,093): improved dichotomous sleep quality (RR 1.8) but no benefit on quantitative sleep measures.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: RCTs showing anxiety reduction and sleep quality improvement.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Janda et al. (2020) systematic review (9 clinical trials): majority reported reduced anxiety; one RCT showed comparable efficacy to oxazepam for GAD with fewer side effects. Ngan & Conduit (2011) RCT (n=41): passionflower tea 1 cup/d for 7 d improved PSQI sleep quality +5.2% vs placebo.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human studies show mood improvement and cognitive enhancement.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Ghazizadeh et al. (2021) meta-analysis (10 RCTs, 6 in quantitative MA): Melissa officinalis reduced anxiety (SMD -1.07, p<0.001) and depression without serious adverse effects. Cases et al. (2011) RCT (n=20): 600 mg/d for 15 d reduced anxiety -18%, insomnia -42%.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Clears senescent cells in animal models, extending healthspan; emerging human interest.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Mayo Clinic long-COVID open-label (n=44): 64% symptom relief; multiple Phase II RCTs recruiting (AFFIRM-LITE, STOP-Sepsis) but no completed DB-RCT results published.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Induces autophagy and extends lifespan in yeast, flies, and mice; mechanistic rationale for aging.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Phase IIa pilot DB-RCT (n=30): positive memory impact in subjective cognitive decline; 2024 DB-RCT (n=37) confirms safety but minimal polyamine changes (Schwarz 2024).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Rejuvenates epigenome and extends lifespan in mice; potential for metabolic disorders.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Rejuvant open-label study shows 8-yr biological age reduction (uncontrolled); ABLE DB-RCT (n=120, NUS) testing Ca-AKG vs placebo ongoing (GeroScience 2023).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Inhibits CD38 enzyme to boost NAD+; preclinical data for sleep and anti-aging effects.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Chamomile extract (1.2% apigenin) DB-RCT shows GAD symptom reduction; no dedicated apigenin-alone RCTs exist. CD38 inhibition mechanism for NAD+ supported preclinically.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Stabilizes mast cells and reduces neuroinflammation in models; potential for allergies and brain health.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: DB-RCT: PEA+luteolin improves post-COVID olfactory recovery; open-label ASD trial (luteolin+quercetin) shows 75% GI improvement (Theoharides 2012). No standalone RCTs.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human randomized and meta-analytic evidence supports taurine for modest improvements in blood pressure and selected cardiometabolic markers; effects vary by dose and baseline risk.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Meta-analysis of RCTs reported blood-pressure and lipid improvements with taurine supplementation (PMID: 32871172). A newer 2025 meta-analysis reported broader cardiometabolic improvements across 34 RCTs (PMID: 41275513).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Improved absorption form; preclinical senolytic effects with dasatinib.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: PK crossover: Quercefit phytosome 20-fold bioavailability vs aglycone; pilot COVID-19 healthcare workers study; limited clinical outcome evidence (Indena 2018).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Multiple human RCTs for CFS/ME including 207-patient trial (CoQ10+NADH combination). Direct NAD+ precursor with fatigue/cognition support. Small effect sizes. Previously misclassified as preclinical.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: DB-RCT crossover (n=26): 31% response vs 8% placebo for CFS; CoQ10+NADH RCT (n=207) significant fatigue impact reduction in ME/CFS (Castro-Marrero 2021).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Multiple human exercise RCTs showing body composition and performance benefits at 2-2.5g/day. Also established as methyl donor for homocysteine reduction (FDA-approved for homocystinuria). Previously misclassified as preclinical.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Multiple human RCTs: 14-wk DB trial in soccer players (2g/d) strength/power gains; 6-wk trial improved body composition in trained men (Cholewa 2018, JISSN).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Multiple meta-analyses support generic astaxanthin for exercise performance (11 RCTs), oxidative stress reduction, skin aging, and lipid profiles. Branded forms (AstaReal) have stronger individual trials. Previously misclassified as preclinical.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: 2024 meta-analysis of 11 RCTs (n=346): significant aerobic exercise performance improvement; additional meta-analyses for lipids, skin aging, blood pressure (Liu 2024).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Multiple human RCTs across cancer prevention (Ki-67 -44%), autism (6 RCTs, n=333), prediabetes, and inflammation. Nrf2 activation and phase II detox enzyme induction confirmed. Broccoli sprout extract is primary source. Previously misclassified as preclinical.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Multiple human RCTs: lung cancer prevention trial Ki-67 -44% (P=0.004); autism meta-analysis of 6 RCTs (n=333) shows irritability improvement; HIV CRP -40% (2024).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human RCT (n=80) shows significant blood pressure reduction. Safety confirmed at 250mg/day. LDL increase noted with monotherapy. Limited trial base (1-2 dedicated trials). Previously misclassified as preclinical.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: DB-RCT (n=80): 250mg/d reduces systolic BP -7.8mmHg (P<0.01), diastolic -7.3mmHg (P<0.001); safety confirmed up to 250mg/d 6-8 wks (Riche 2014, ECAM).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Strong RCT evidence for functional dyspepsia (n=247 DB-RCT) and cholesterol reduction (9-trial meta-analysis: TC -17.6, LDL -14.9 mg/dL). Cynarin and chlorogenic acid are active compounds. Previously misclassified as preclinical.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: DB-RCT (n=247) significant functional dyspepsia symptom reduction; lipid meta-analysis of 9 RCTs (n=702): TC -17.6 mg/dL, LDL -14.9 mg/dL (Sahebkar 2017).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human DB-RCTs for functional dyspepsia (56% marked improvement) and H. pylori eradication (56% vs 4% placebo). GutGard is a standardized DGL extract. Mucosal protection mechanism confirmed. Previously misclassified as preclinical.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: GutGard DB-RCT: 56% marked improvement in functional dyspepsia vs placebo; H. pylori DB-RCT (n=107): 56% eradication vs 4% placebo at 60 days (Raveendra 2012).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Multiple human RCTs for gastric ulcer (n=258, 61% improvement), H. pylori eradication (86.8% vs 70.9%), and GI mucosal health. Long safety record in Japan as prescription drug (Promac). Previously misclassified as preclinical.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: RCT (n=258): 150mg/day 4 wks shows 61% gastric ulcer symptom improvement; meta-analysis polaprezinc H. pylori eradication 86.8% vs 70.9% (Nutrients 2022).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Strong systematic review evidence supports Boswellia serrata for osteoarthritis pain and stiffness. Meta-analysis of 7 RCTs (n=545) shows significant WOMAC/VAS improvements. 5-LOX and leukotriene synthesis inhibition is the confirmed mechanism. Previously misclassified as preclinical.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Meta-analysis of 7 RCTs (n=545) significant OA pain/stiffness reduction (WOMAC, VAS); Yu 2024 multi-center DB-RCT improvement within 5 days, Front Pharmacol.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Meta-analysis of 20 RCTs (n=1823) shows significant MMSE improvement for Alzheimer's. However, US Phase II trial failed primary endpoint and Chinese trial quality is variable. AChE inhibitor mechanism is established. Previously misclassified as preclinical.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Meta-analysis of 20 RCTs (n=1823): significant MMSE improvement at 8/12/16 wks for Alzheimer's; US Phase II trial negative on primary endpoint (PLoS ONE 2013).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Multiple human DB-RCTs showing aerobic capacity improvements (VO2peak, ventilatory threshold). Both Cs-4 (fermented) and C. militaris tested. ATP production and oxygen utilization mechanisms supported. Previously misclassified as preclinical.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: DB-RCT: Cs-4 3g/d 6 wks improves VO2peak and ventilatory threshold in elderly; C. militaris RCT +4.8 ml/kg/min VO2max after 3 wks (P=0.042, Hirsch 2017).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Used to support stomach acid; mechanistic rationale exists but no formal human trials.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Pilot study (n=6): 1500mg lowers gastric pH from 5.2 to 0.6 within 6 min in PPI-induced hypochlorhydria; reacidification lasts ~73 min (Yago 2013, Mol Pharm).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Strong systematic review evidence supports saffron extract for mild-to-moderate depression, comparable to SSRIs (fluoxetine, citalopram). Meta-analysis of 8 RCTs confirms antidepressant effect. Crocin and safranal are active compounds. Previously misclassified as preclinical.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Meta-analysis of 8 RCTs: saffron comparable to SSRIs for depression (Akhondzadeh et al.); Lopresti 2024 14mg BID significantly reduces HAM-D scores vs placebo, Nutr Rev.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Extensive systematic review evidence (28 RCTs) shows significant glycemic improvements in type 2 diabetes. HbA1c reduction of -0.55%. ADA notes insufficient evidence for routine use despite statistical significance. Clinical relevance debated. Previously misclassified as preclinical.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Meta-analysis of 28 RCTs: significant FPG, HbA1c (-0.55%), insulin, HOMA-IR reductions in T2DM; ADA notes insufficient evidence for routine use (Asbaghi 2020).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Cochrane review and meta-analyses confirm acetyl-L-carnitine efficacy for peripheral neuropathic pain (20.2% reduction vs placebo). Established mitochondrial support mechanism. 6-12 month treatment duration needed. Previously misclassified as preclinical.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Meta-analysis of 4 RCTs (n=523): 20.2% pain reduction in diabetic/HIV neuropathy (P<0.0001); Cochrane review confirms benefit at 6-12 months, Li et al. PLoS ONE.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Rapidly evolving evidence — 12 human RCTs (n=513) confirm NAD+ elevation. Clinical outcomes (walking speed, sleep) emerging but most endpoints not significantly different from placebo yet. Regulatory status varies by country. Previously misclassified as preclinical.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: 2024 meta-analysis of 12 RCTs (n=513): significant blood NAD+ elevation; DB-RCT in elderly (n=60) maintained walking speed + improved sleep quality (Aging Cell 2024).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Small human RCTs (n=34-64) for cognition and muscle function. Mitochondrial biogenesis support mechanism. Evidence is promising but sample sizes remain small. Previously misclassified as preclinical.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: DB-RCT (n=64): 21.5mg/d PQQ 12 wks improves memory, attention, cognitive function in middle-aged/elderly; MCI trial (n=34) shows brain metabolism benefits (2024).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Extensive human RCT base exists with meta-analyses, but 2024 systematic review concludes no conclusive clinical evidence of benefit. Statistically significant effects on some biomarkers (CRP, lipids) but clinical relevance uncertain. Bioavailability is limiting factor. Previously misclassified as preclinical.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Multiple meta-analyses of human RCTs for T2DM (inflammation, oxidative stress), CVD (anti-inflammatory), and lipid profiles; 2024 review: no conclusive clinical benefit established.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Exceptionally strong human evidence base — 103 RCTs (n=7216) with high-credibility evidence for glycemic, inflammatory (CRP), lipid (HDL), and body weight outcomes. Bioavailability-enhanced forms required for clinical effect. Previously misclassified as preclinical.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Comprehensive meta-analysis of 103 RCTs (n=7216): high-credibility evidence for FBS, CRP, HDL, weight; significant effects on 23/42 outcomes (Bagherniya 2024).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Very strong human evidence — 50 RCTs (n=4150) with significant metabolic improvements in FPG, LDL-C, TC, TG. AMPK activation is confirmed mechanism. Generic forms lack standardization compared to berberine HCl. Previously misclassified as preclinical.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Meta-analysis of 50 RCTs (n=4150): significant FPG reduction (-0.515 mmol/L), LDL-C, TC, TG improvements; AMPK activation mechanism confirmed (Front Pharmacol 2025).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: 60+ clinical studies. Meta-analysis confirmed efficacy for periodontitis. World's most studied probiotic for oral health.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Borrell Garcia et al. (2021) pilot RCT (n=27 adolescents): L. reuteri DSM 17938 + ATCC PTA 5289 lozenges for 28 d showed trends toward reduced S. mutans and improved plaque/gingivitis indices (J Int Oral Health 13:337). Evidence base is predominantly small pilot studies; larger confirmatory RCTs for oral health needed.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: 30+ studies including 8 clinical trials. RCTs show 28-35% improvement in cognitive tests (TMT-B). 1.5g dose comparable to 8g citrulline malate for vasodilation. Patented by Nutrition21.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Kalman et al. (2016) two crossover RCTs (healthy males): Nitrosigine 1500 mg/d improved Trail Making Test B (cognitive flexibility) at 3 d and 14 d of supplementation vs placebo (Nutrients 8:736). Evidence base is limited to small crossover studies; larger chronic supplementation data needed.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: 25+ human clinical studies. MIT/Malaysian government research collaboration. RCTs show 16.4% higher testosterone, 32.3% lower cortisol. Standardized to 40% glycosaponins, 22% eurypeptides.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Tambi et al. (2012) RCT (n=109, men 30-55): LJ100 200 mg/d for 12 wk improved erectile function (IIEF), libido, fat mass, and muscular force vs placebo (ECAM 2012:429268). Ismail et al. (2012) open-label (n=76, late-onset hypogonadism): 200 mg/d for 12 wk — 90.8% achieved normal testosterone at endpoint. Note: n=76 study was open-label, not randomized.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: High-potency extract (35% withanolide glycosides). 2024 RCT showed significant cortisol reduction and testosterone increase. 60-120mg effective dose. 72% sleep improvement vs 29% placebo.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Lopresti et al. (2019) RCT (n=60): Shoden 240 mg/d (35% withanolide glycosides) for 60 d reduced morning cortisol -23%, improved DASS-21 stress/anxiety, sleep quality (72% vs 29% placebo), increased DHEA-S and testosterone (Medicine 98:e17186). Tharakan et al. (2024) RCT (n=60): 60-120 mg/d — 59% greater anxiety reduction vs placebo (Heliyon 10:e37534).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Multiple RCTs: 8-week study (n=138) showed greater strength gains. 12-week trial (n=120) improved testosterone and sexual function. 300-600mg dose. Standardized to 50% Fenuside.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Rao et al. (2016) RCT (n=120, men 43-70): Testofen 600 mg/d for 12 wk increased total/free testosterone, improved sexual function (DISF-SR), reduced androgen deficiency symptoms (Aging Male 19:134). Steels et al. (2011) RCT (n=60, men 25-52): 600 mg/d for 6 wk improved libido (Phytother Res 25:1294).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Multiple RCTs. 2025 study: 6-week use improved cognition, working memory, reaction time. 2024 RCT: 21-day use improved deep sleep. Only magnesium form shown to efficiently cross blood-brain barrier.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Surber et al. (2025) RCT (n=100, 18-45 yrs): Magtein 2 g/d for 6 wk improved cognitive performance (reduced brain cognitive age by 7.5 yr), working memory, reaction time, and HRV vs placebo (Front Nutr 12:1729164). Zhang et al. (2022) RCT (n=109): Magtein formula for 30 d improved all Clinical Memory Test subcategories (Nutrients 14:5235).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: 100+ human clinical studies. RCT for IBS showed significant symptom improvement at 6g/day. Truly regulating fiber - helps both constipation and diarrhea.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Niv et al. (2016) RCT (n=121, IBS): Sunfiber PHGG 6 g/d for 12 wk improved bloating and bloating+gas composite vs placebo (World J Gastroenterol 22:5235). Takahashi et al. (2019) RCT (n=44): PHGG 5 g/d for 3 mo improved stool form and increased Bifidobacterium (Nutrients 11:2170).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: RCT (n=50) showed significant hair/nail improvement within 90 days. 500mg daily dose. Solubilized keratin form for bioavailability.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Beer et al. (2014) RCT (n=50 women, 90 d): Cynatine HNS 500 mg solubilized keratin improved hair shedding, growth rate, strength, luster, nail hardness, and skin appearance vs placebo (ScientificWorldJournal 2014:641723). Only 1 published RCT; co-administered vitamins (zinc, biotin, B-complex) confound keratin's independent contribution.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: 3 human clinical trials with 300+ participants. Fast-acting: results in 5 days at 250mg. Plant-based. Used in Tylenol supplements, Schiff Move Free, Platinum Performance (2025).
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Rao et al. (2019) RCT (n=150, knee OA, 12 wk): TamaFlex 250 mg/d improved WOMAC total/pain/stiffness/function from day 5, comparable to naproxen; reduced cartilage degradation biomarker CTX-II vs placebo (J Med Food 22:893). Targets both COX-2 and 5-LOX inflammatory pathways.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Leucine metabolite. Multiple meta-analyses confirm efficacy for muscle preservation, especially in elderly/catabolic states. 3g/day typical dose. Free acid form faster acting.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Nissen et al. (1996) landmark RCT (n=41): HMB 3 g/d during resistance training increased FFM +1.21 kg vs +0.40 kg placebo and reduced muscle proteolysis (J Appl Physiol 81:2095). Wilson et al. (2014) RCT (n=40 trained men): HMB-FA 3 g/d for 12 wk increased lean mass, squat/bench/deadlift strength, and power vs placebo (Eur J Appl Physiol 114:2217).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Patented mango leaf extract (>60% mangiferin). RCTs show cognitive benefits comparable to caffeine without stimulant side effects. Often combined with quercetin for synergy.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Wightman et al. (2020) crossover RCT (n=70): single Zynamite 300 mg dose improved cognitive accuracy (attention, working memory) and reduced mental fatigue within 1 h vs placebo. Gelabert-Rebato et al. (2019) crossover RCT (n=40): Zynamite improved exercise performance and reduced muscle damage markers. 7 published RCTs total.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Proprietary L. plantarum strain. Multiple RCTs show improved sleep quality, lower morning cortisol, balanced stress response. Part of Probi Mental Health product line.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Berggren et al. (2011) RCT (n=272): L. plantarum HEAL9 + L. paracasei 8700:2 1B CFU/d for 12 wk — fewer cold symptom days (6.2 vs 8.6 d, p<0.05) and shorter episodes (Eur J Nutr 50:203). Lazou Ahren et al. (2022) RCT (n=898): replicated — lower total cold symptom score (73.1 vs 117.0, p<0.001) (J Funct Foods 88:104901).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Branded lutein/zeaxanthin isomer complex with human studies in visual function and eye-strain-related endpoints; many studies are industry sponsored.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Stringham et al. (2017) LAMA II RCT (n=59): Lutemax 2020 (lutein + RR/RS-zeaxanthin isomers) 24 mg/d for 6 mo improved MPOD, visual processing speed, and composite memory vs placebo (Nutrients 9:1246). Nolan et al. (2018) RCT (n=105, 12 mo): significant MPOD increase and chromatic contrast threshold improvement.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Purified shilajit ingredient with controlled human data in male reproductive and androgen-related outcomes.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Pandit et al. (2016) RCT (n=75 men, 90 d): PrimaVie 250 mg BID increased total testosterone +20.45% and free testosterone +19.14% vs placebo (Andrologia 48:570). Sharma et al. (2003) RCT (n=26 infertile men): shilajit 200 mg/d for 90 d increased sperm count by 61.4% and motility by 12.4%.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Comparative human pharmacokinetic studies suggest faster uptake versus some standard vitamin C forms; evidence base is narrower than for generic vitamin C.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Moyad et al. (2008) parallel-group PK study (n=40, 4 arms x 10): PureWay-C showed higher 24-h plasma vitamin C retention vs standard ascorbic acid, Ester-C, and calcium ascorbate. Only 1 published comparative study; limited clinical endpoint data beyond bioavailability.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Consistent evidence for mild-to-moderate depression contexts, but clinically significant CYP/P-gp drug interaction risk requires caution.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Cochrane review (CD000448) reports superiority to placebo in mild-to-moderate depression in many trials with better tolerability than some antidepressants.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Clinical data are broad and endpoint-specific; strongest support is for antioxidant biology rather than universal disease-risk reduction.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: NIH ODS and pooled human trials support antioxidant and deficiency-correction roles with context-dependent clinical outcomes.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Supported in targeted populations and in combination eye-health formulations (e.g., AREDS family formulas).
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: AREDS/AREDS2 programs established role of carotenoid-containing formulas in progression risk reduction for selected AMD populations.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human evidence supports magnesium supplementation for several outcomes in deficiency-risk and condition-specific groups.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Multiple meta-analyses report modest blood-pressure and migraine-prevention benefits, with form- and dose-dependent effects.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Broad human evidence base in bone-health settings, especially when paired with vitamin D and adequate baseline intake assessment.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: NIH ODS and pooled trials support BMD outcomes, with strongest utility in at-risk populations and adequate adherence.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Controlled trials support specific indications such as pregnancy-related nausea and selected PMS outcomes.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Clinical guidance and human trials support B6 in nausea-of-pregnancy protocols and B-complex homocysteine management.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human evidence strongest in deficiency and neuropathy-related contexts; benfotiamine data support symptom-oriented use.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Clinical studies support thiamine repletion and benfotiamine use in neuropathy-related metabolic settings.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human interventional evidence exists but is more limited than major vitamins; strongest support is biochemical and deficiency correction.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Human studies and biochemical evidence support coenzyme A pathway relevance and metabolic support roles.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Best human evidence is in migraine prevention protocols, including clinical guideline support in some regions.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Controlled trials and meta-analyses report reduced migraine frequency with high-dose riboflavin protocols.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human intervention data support blood-pressure effects primarily in low-intake or high-sodium contexts.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Dietary intervention and supplementation studies support BP modulation, with safety constraints in renal impairment.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Strong deficiency-prevention evidence, especially in pregnancy and low-intake populations; excess iodine risk noted.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: NIH ODS and public-health interventions support iodine's role in thyroid outcomes and developmental health.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human supplementation evidence is more limited than calcium/magnesium but supports deficiency correction and cofactor biology.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Evidence is strongest for essentiality and deficiency correction; interventional outcomes are modest and population-specific.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human evidence supports copper as an essential cofactor with clinically meaningful effects in deficiency states.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: NIH ODS references essentiality and deficiency manifestations; supplementation should avoid excess exposure.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Evidence is primarily essentiality and deficiency correction; supplementation utility is context-specific.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Human evidence supports physiologic necessity; targeted supplementation is generally reserved for low-intake or clinical contexts.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Strong evidence base for LDL-lowering and bowel-regularity outcomes; supports cardiometabolic and GI-health use.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: FDA-authorized CHD claim for soluble fiber from psyllium (21 CFR 101.81) and multiple meta-analyses showing LDL reduction.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Emerging but growing human evidence supports bone and inflammatory biomarker effects at supplemental doses.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Small human trials report favorable shifts in selected inflammatory and hormone-related biomarkers.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human evidence indicates immunomodulatory effects, but heterogeneity and methodological variability are substantial.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Systematic review/meta-analysis of human studies found favorable immune-marker changes with high heterogeneity (PMID: 37952511).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Systematic review evidence suggests modest pain/sinus-related benefits with generally acceptable safety.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Systematic review/meta-analysis of 54 studies found small but significant oral pain benefit and potential sinusitis utility (PMID: 37157782).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human trials and meta-analyses support oral-health applications, with results influenced by extract standardization.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Recent meta-analysis of RCTs found propolis mouthwash reduced plaque/gingival inflammation outcomes (PMID: 41003407).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Evidence suggests modest, formulation-dependent effects for common-cold outcomes; heterogeneity across preparations is substantial.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Cochrane review (CD000530) and later meta-analyses indicate mixed but signal-positive findings in selected products and regimens.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Systematic-review-level evidence supports honey for acute upper respiratory symptom relief, especially cough severity and duration.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: BMJ Evidence-Based Medicine systematic review (2020/2021) reported better symptom relief with honey vs usual care for URTI symptoms.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Early human and preclinical evidence suggest hormonal-metabolism effects; robust phase-3 style data are limited.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Most evidence remains preclinical or small early-phase human studies.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Generic shilajit evidence is less standardized than purified branded forms; preclinical and early-stage data predominate.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Mechanistic and animal studies support mitochondrial and antioxidant pathways; composition variability is a major caveat.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human RCTs and meta-analyses support pain-reduction outcomes; effect sizes vary by pain indication and study quality.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: 2023 systematic review/meta-analysis of double-blind RCTs (PMID: 36986081) reported significant pain reduction versus comparators.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Extensive human evidence supports creatine monohydrate for strength, lean mass, and high-intensity performance. Evidence is strongest when paired with resistance training and adequate dosing.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Recent 2024 meta-analyses show improved strength and body-composition outcomes when creatine is paired with resistance training (PMIDs: 39519498, 39074168).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human evidence supports enteric-coated peppermint oil for IBS symptom relief. Benefits are formulation-dependent and strongest for GI-targeted products.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Meta-analyses and systematic reviews show peppermint oil improves IBS outcomes versus placebo in multiple RCTs (e.g., PMIDs: 24060759, 31470006).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: LGG is one of the most studied probiotic strains with strain-specific human outcomes, especially for antibiotic-associated diarrhea prevention contexts.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Systematic review/meta-analysis found LGG reduced risk of antibiotic-associated diarrhea in relevant populations (PMID: 24780641).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: BB-12 has multiple human strain-specific trials with strongest consistency in selected GI and immune outcomes; effects vary by population and protocol.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Clinical literature shows strain-specific BB-12 human outcomes with strongest support in selected pediatric GI settings and mixed effects in other indications (PMIDs: 31797399, 32059116).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: HN019 has human strain-specific evidence, particularly for bowel function and transit outcomes in constipation-prone groups.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Meta-analysis and randomized trials report HN019-related improvements in transit-time and bowel-function outcomes in constipation-prone subgroups (PMIDs: 23922468, 29227175).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human evidence is emerging and mixed; some trials show modest stress/sleep improvements, but effect certainty remains lower than top-tier ingredients.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Systematic review of placebo-controlled human trials found limited evidence for stress and very limited evidence for sleep benefits of oral GABA (PMID: 33041752).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Systematic-review-level evidence supports chamomile for selected sleep and anxiety outcomes, with moderate heterogeneity by population and preparation.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: A 2024 systematic review/meta-analysis reported sleep-related improvements in several clinical contexts, with heterogeneity across populations and preparations (PMID: 39106912).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Nitrate-rich beetroot has systematic-review evidence for small-to-moderate improvements in exercise performance and modest blood-pressure reductions.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Meta-analyses report small-to-moderate ergogenic and vascular effects from dietary nitrate/beetroot interventions in adults (PMIDs: 33554654, 34965876).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human systematic reviews support protein supplementation, including whey-rich interventions, for improving lean mass and strength outcomes when paired with resistance training.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Systematic review/meta-regression in healthy adults found protein supplementation improved RET-induced gains in strength and fat-free mass (PMID: 28698222). Recent meta-analysis in older adults found strongest effects when combined with resistance training (PMID: 39303495).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Citicoline has human trial support for selected cognition outcomes, with stronger signals in older adults and impairment-related contexts; evidence quality remains mixed across populations.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Randomized placebo-controlled trial in healthy older adults showed memory-related benefits (PMID: 33978188). Systematic review/meta-analysis found positive cognitive effects but overall study quality was variable (PMID: 36678257).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Alpha-GPC has multiple human trials and meta-analytic coverage, with strongest effects in cognitive-impairment and combination-therapy contexts; healthy-adult evidence is emerging.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Systematic review/meta-analysis on adult-onset cognitive dysfunctions reported significant benefits in cognition and function outcomes across included trials (PMID: 36683513).

key_endpoints: GI adverse effects during antibiotic therapy ↓
backed_clinical_studies.notes: Strain-level yeast probiotic evidence is strongest for prevention of antibiotic-associated diarrhea, with additional supportive evidence in selected GI contexts.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Updated systematic review/meta-analysis across 21 randomized trials found reduced antibiotic-associated diarrhea risk with S. boulardii (PMID: 26216624).
